pr.title: Decompile 9 fighter, library, game, and menu functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Merged multi-system decomp PR. The supplied diff replaces stubs with C for eight visible functions: `ftCo_800C0134`, `fn_800DA1D8`, `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim`, `pl_80037DF4`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `mnName_80238A04`, and `fn_8001F06C`, plus related header, prototype, include, and `THPDecComp` layout fixes. The PR body says nine functions and lists `un_80307828` in `toy.c`, but `toy.c` is absent from the provided changed-files/diff slice, so that item is only body-evidenced here. Review feedback caught an unscoped `#pragma dont_inline on` in `mnname.c`; the final diff wraps it with `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`.

key_files.notes: Decompiled collision-animation reset/update logic; uses scoped `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off`.
Decompiled CatchWait/grab setup, callback assignment, grabbed-joint storage, grab effect spawn, and victim update.
Changed prototype from `UNK_RET fn_800DA1D8(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Decompiled Crazy Hand damage animation completion logic and added `mp/mplib.h` for `mpFloorGetRight`.
Decompiled Popo aerial up-special throw animation, including animation-complete knockback call and Nana availability follow-up check.
Decompiled attack-stat counter update for totals, attack type, thrown items, aerials, specials, and two unknown counters.
Decompiled 256-slot game event dispatch loop using input masks and event handler checks.
Changed helper from void wrapper to `int` return, returning `pl_80039418(arg0, arg1)` for use by `fn_8017F1B8`.
Header prototype updated to match the new `int` return type.
Replaced unknown prototype with `void fn_8016FFD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, int, int)`.
Decompiled THP playback/decode counter update inside an interrupt-disabled critical section.
Refined `THPDecComp` fields at 0x68, 0x6C, 0x108, and 0x10C and changed `fn_8001F06C` to `s32 fn_8001F06C(THPDecComp* data)`.
Decompiled name-entry slot animation updater; final diff scopes `#pragma dont_inline on` with push/pop after review.
Changed prototype to `void mnName_80238A04(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 target, u8 flag)`.

what_changed: Replaced `/// #` stubs with concrete C implementations for the eight functions visible in the diff: `ftCo_800C0134`, `fn_800DA1D8`, `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim`, `pl_80037DF4`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `mnName_80238A04`, and `fn_8001F06C`.
Updated decompiled-function headers from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures for `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `fn_8001F06C`, and `mnName_80238A04`.
Fixed supporting game prototypes: `fn_8016F160` now returns `int` and returns `pl_80039418`, and `fn_8016FFD4` now takes `struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*` plus two `int` arguments.
Expanded `THPDecComp` layout by converting padding into typed fields: signed fields at 0x68 and 0x6C, plus signed counters at 0x108 and 0x10C.
Added `mp/mplib.h` in Crazy Hand init code to support `mpFloorGetRight` used by `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`.
Scoped compiler pragmas in decompiled functions: `ftcolanim.c` uses push/pop around optimizer settings, and `mnname.c` was fixed after review to wrap `#pragma dont_inline on` with push/pop.

Verification evidence is mixed in the slice: the PR body claims all 9 functions were 100% matched by an overnight script, while the visible decomp-dev bot report shows +844 bytes and, at that run, 4 new 100% matches plus 4 near-matches.
smart_moves: Typed the prototypes alongside the decomp work instead of leaving `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, which made call sites such as `fn_8017F1B8` able to branch on real `int` return values.
Used function-local pragma scoping for match-sensitive code, preventing optimizer or inlining settings from leaking into unrelated functions.
Refined `THPDecComp` fields rather than accessing raw padding, making `fn_8001F06C` express frame/decode counters directly while preserving offsets.
Matched interrupt-sensitive THP control flow with `OSDisableInterrupts`, a shared `end:` label, and guaranteed `OSRestoreInterrupts` before returning.
Preserved match-sensitive source shape where needed: `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(40)`, nested scopes, local redeclarations, explicit casts, and a local copy of `union Struct2070`.
Kept fighter callback/state details explicit in `fn_800DA1D8`, including `accessory1_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`, grabbed joint storage, effect spawn, and victim update.
decomp_lessons: Function-specific pragmas must be surrounded by `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop`; an unscoped `#pragma dont_inline on` affects every later function in the file.
When a matched function depends on a helper's return value, update the helper implementation and declaration together rather than casting around an unknown or void prototype.
Struct padding should be converted to typed fields when a decompiled function proves offsets, signedness, or comparisons; this PR used `s32` for THP fields compared against zero.
Game event dispatch code can require repeated mask calls and explicit `(u8)`/`(u32)` casts to reproduce original codegen.
Fighter state-transition decomp often needs exact callback slots, JObj selection, and move-state storage, not just the high-level `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` call.
Cross-check PR-body match claims against changed files and bot reports; here `un_80307828`/`toy.c` appears in the body but not in the provided diff slice.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_*` denotes common fighter code, `ftCh_*` Crazy Hand, `ftPp_*` Popo, and `ftNn_*` Nana/Ice Climbers partner code.
`gm_*` and `fn_8016*`/`fn_8017*` names are retained for game-mode/event functions that still lack semantic names.
`mnName_*` is used for name-entry menu functions; `lbmthp` contains THP video-library functions; `pl_*` names are used for player/stat tracking code.
Unresolved functions keep address-based names such as `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, and `fn_8001F06C`, with address comments in headers like `/* 0DA1D8 */`.
Local fighter naming follows project style: `Fighter_GObj* gobj`, `Fighter* fp`, HSD joint pointers `jobj`/`jobj2`, and data attributes as `da` or `attrs`.
Unknown fields retain offset-like names such as `unk_108`, `unk_10C`, `x221B_b7`, and `mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0` until stronger semantic evidence exists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)` in `fn_800DA1D8` and `PAD_STACK(40)` in `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim` were used for stack-shape matching.
`#pragma push`/`#pragma auto_inline off`/`#pragma global_optimizer off` were applied only around `ftCo_800C0134`.
`#pragma push`/`#pragma dont_inline on`/`#pragma pop` were applied around `mnName_80238A04` after review.
`fn_8001F06C` uses a `goto end` early-exit path so interrupt restoration occurs at a single matching epilogue.
Visible casts such as `(u8) fn_8016F180(i)`, `(u32) fn_8016F160(0, i)`, `(u8) target`, and `(u8) flag` were kept in source to match comparisons and masks.
Nested blocks and local redeclarations in `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim` likely help preserve original register/stack lifetimes while checking Nana availability.
`ftCh_Damage2_Anim` uses an assignment expression inside `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments for `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z = ftCh_Init_804DA070`, preserving ordering visible in the matched source.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: `#pragma dont_inline on` lacked a preceding `#pragma push` and trailing `#pragma pop`, so it would incorrectly affect all later functions in the file.
review_feedback.resolution: johnwinston replied that it was fixed; the final diff shows `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, `mnName_80238A04`, then `#pragma pop`.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Intermediate GALE01 report showed matched code 59.42% (+0.02%, +844 bytes), with 4 new 100% matches and 4 visible functions still near-matching at that run.
review_feedback.resolution: PR body later states all 9 functions were 100% matched by overnight script; the slice does not provide a later bot table for the body-only `toy.c` item.

follow_up_queries: Search for other unscoped `#pragma dont_inline`, `#pragma auto_inline`, or optimizer pragmas and wrap function-local uses with `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`.
Audit `THPDecComp` uses around offsets 0x68, 0x6C, 0x108, and 0x10C to derive better semantic field names for playback/frame counters.
Review `gm_16AE.h`, `gm_16F1.h`, and nearby game-event functions for more `UNK_RET` prototypes that should become typed return values or typed state-pointer arguments.
Verify whether `un_80307828` in `toy.c` landed in another commit or PR, because it is in this PR body but absent from the supplied changed-files/diff slice.
Use `pl_80037DF4` as a reference pattern for decompiling other player action/stat counter functions using `union Struct2070` flag fields.
Compare `ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim` and `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim` for reusable Ice Climbers partner/Nana follow-up control flow.
